_clinical_notes: Shin splints (MTSS) = diffuse medial tibial pain that warms up with activity. Key differential: stress fracture = pinpoint tenderness at one spot, pain with hop test, does NOT warm up with activity. Compartment syndrome (acute or chronic exertional): tightness/pressure that builds rapidly with exercise, doesn't resolve with rest, possible foot numbness = seek care. Grades for MTSS: mild (pain after activity only), moderate (pain during + after), severe (pain at rest or with walking).

pain_character.prompt: How would you describe the shin pain?

options.label: Dull ache along a wide area of the shin — comes on during or after running
Sharp or throbbing pain at one specific spot on the shin
Intense tightness or pressure that builds up during exercise

onset_timing.prompt: When does the pain occur during activity?

options.label: Only after I stop — fine while running
Starts mid-run but I can push through
Hurts from the start of running / even walking

warmup_check.prompt: Does the pain ease up after you've been running for a few minutes (warm-up effect)?

options.label: Yes, it warms up and gets better mid-run
No, it stays the same or gets worse

hop_test.prompt: Try a single-leg hop on the injured leg. Does it cause sharp pain in the shin?

options.label: No sharp pain — just general soreness

compartment_check.prompt: After you stop exercising, does the tightness/pressure go away within about 15-30 minutes?

options.label: Yes, resolves fairly quickly with rest
No — persists for hours or I have foot numbness / tingling

grade_severe.prompt: Does it hurt when you're just walking around or standing?

options.label: Yes, even walking or standing is painful
Mostly with running, walking is okay

outcome.seek_care_reason: Point tenderness at one spot on the shin combined with pain on hopping suggests a possible stress fracture. This needs imaging (X-ray + possible MRI/bone scan) to confirm.
Persistent tightness or pressure that doesn't resolve with rest, especially with foot numbness, may indicate compartment syndrome. This needs prompt medical evaluation.
